topic: An archer demonstrating the Khatra technique upon releasing an arrow, slow-motion side view.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Side-angle close-up of the archer nocking and drawing the bow, initiating the Khatra technique.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Slow-motion side view of the release as the arrow departs, showcasing the Khatra action.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Side-angle view of the archer watching the arrow in flight, finalizing the Khatra demonstration.
parsed_generation.narration: An archer demonstrates the Khatra technique in a three-shot sequence from a slow-motion side view. The draw, the release in slow motion, and the arrow in flight combine to convey precision and control.

feedback: The sequence centers on an archer and references the Khatra technique, but it diverges from the gold standard by using three shots and not explicitly illustrating the two-part Khatra motion (draw release with a snapping bow hand and outward bow rotation to clear the riser). To tighten alignment, structure two distinct, timestamped beats that depict the draw/rapid release and then the outward rotation and arrow flight, with precise camera cues (e.g., macro for release, tracking for flight). Add concrete cues about lighting and texture to heighten cinematic impact.
strengths: Clear focus on the archer and technique
Consistent mention of Khatra across shots
fixes: Adopt the two-shot cadence from the gold standard (00:00-00:04 draw/release, 00:04-00:08 khatra rotation and flight)
